module1.clean_text: A [AGE] male presented to Chigateri General Hospital in [DATE] with joint pain for one year and generalized weakness for 20 days. He reported progressive, painless blurring of vision over the past two months, more pronounced in the right eye. There was no history of photophobia, lachrymation, or trauma. He has been managing diabetes mellitus for six years with insulin, metformin, and pioglitazone. His parents had a third-degree consanguineous marriage, and his sibling is unaffected. A maternal cousin has similar skin lesions. His mother did not receive antenatal care and had a domicile delivery. Immunization history is inconsistent. There was no history of palpitation, lower limb swelling, syncope, reduced urine output, hypertension, congenital heart disease, kidney disease, hypothyroidism, or other illnesses. The patient appeared poorly built and undernourished. Vital signs were normal, with no pallor, icterus, cyanosis, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or edema. His body mass index was 15.4 kg/m2, middle upper arm circumference was 16.5 cm, and head circumference was 51.5 cm. He had large prominent eyes, coarse facies, malocclusion of the teeth with hyperdontia, dermatitis neglecta with acanthosis nigricans on all sides of the neck, a fissured tongue, and enlarged tonsils. Subungual hyperkeratosis was noted in his fingernails, and acanthosis nigricans was present on the dorsum of the hand extending to the forearm. His toenails showed onychodystrophy and pincer nail. Clinically, his liver was enlarged, and the examination of all other systems was unremarkable.

phenopacket.input: A 19-year-old male presented to Chigateri General Hospital in April 2020 with joint pain for one year and generalized weakness for 20 days. He reported progressive, painless blurring of vision over the past two months, more pronounced in the right eye. There was no history of photophobia, lachrymation, or trauma. He has been managing diabetes mellitus for six years with insulin, metformin, and pioglitazone. His parents had a third-degree consanguineous marriage, and his sibling is unaffected. A maternal cousin has similar skin lesions. His mother did not receive antenatal care and had a domicile delivery. Immunization history is inconsistent. There was no history of palpitation, lower limb swelling, syncope, reduced urine output, hypertension, congenital heart disease, kidney disease, hypothyroidism, or other illnesses. The patient appeared poorly built and undernourished. Vital signs were normal, with no pallor, icterus, cyanosis, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or edema. His body mass index was 15.4 kg/m2, middle upper arm circumference was 16.5 cm, and head circumference was 51.5 cm. He had large prominent eyes, coarse facies, malocclusion of the teeth with hyperdontia, dermatitis neglecta with acanthosis nigricans on all sides of the neck, a fissured tongue, and enlarged tonsils. Subungual hyperkeratosis was noted in his fingernails, and acanthosis nigricans was present on the dorsum of the hand extending to the forearm. His toenails showed onychodystrophy and pincer nail. Clinically, his liver was enlarged, and the examination of all other systems was unremarkable.
